answer:
We are given \( f(x) = ax^6 - bx^4 + x - 1 \) and \( f(2) = 5 \).  
Notice that the even-powered terms \( ax^6 \) and \( -bx^4 \) will give the same value for \( x = 2 \) and \( x = -2 \), so the difference between \( f(2) \) and \( f(-2) \) comes only from the linear term \( x \).  

Thus, \( f(2) = (a2^6 - b2^4) + 2 - 1 = (a2^6 - b2^4) + 1 = 5 \), so \( a2^6 - b2^4 = 4 \).  
Then \( f(-2) = (a2^6 - b2^4) - 2 - 1 = 4 - 3 = 1 \).  

\boxed
